Class FinalEntrantListAdapter

ArrayAdapter
com.example.codebase.FinalEntrantListAdapter
public class FinalEntrantListAdapter extends ArrayAdapter
invalid reference 
ArrayAdapter
that displays a list of entrants identified by device ID, resolving each device ID to a name and email via an asynchronous Firestore lookup.

Each item in the list is a device ID string. While the Firestore fetch is in flight, the name field shows "Loading…" and the email field shows the raw device ID as a placeholder. Once the user document is retrieved, both fields are updated with the stored name and email values, falling back to "Unknown User" or the device ID respectively if those fields are absent.

View recycling via convertView is supported; stale "Loading…" text may briefly appear on recycled views before the new Firestore response arrives.

      public FinalEntrantListAdapter(Context context, ArrayList<String> entrants)

  • Method Details

    • getView

      @NonNull public View getView(int position, @Nullable View convertView, @NonNull ViewGroup parent)
      Returns a view for the entrant at the given position, inflating final_entrant_list_item if no recycled view is available.

      The view is initially populated with placeholder text, then updated asynchronously once the Firestore user document for the device ID is fetched. If the device ID is null or empty the Firestore fetch is skipped and the placeholders remain.

      Parameters:
      position - The index of the item within the adapter's data set.
      convertView - A recycled view to reuse, or null if none is available.
      parent - The parent
      invalid reference 
      ViewGroup
      that this view will be attached to.
      Returns:
      The populated list item
      invalid reference 
      View
      .
